entry.title: Global User ID and SAP IAS: Essentials for AI integration in SAP landscape
entry.content:
Introduction

Modern companies are increasingly using SAP across multiple products as their operations are becoming more cloud-based, diversified and advanced with AI. In these environments, consistent identity management for each user is required, and it is important to accurately identify the same person across multiple systems and to provide appropriate authentication and authorization. SAP’s AI Digital Assistant, Joule, and SAP Task Center also require consistent identity management.

This guide describes the role and mechanism of “Global User ID”, an important feature of SAP’s cloud foundation, SAP Cloud Identity Services, as well as how to configure SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication (SAP IAS) to support its implementation. In particular, it focuses on how to integrate user information and enable single sign-on in collaboration with key SAP products, such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA, and introduces practical steps and notes.

 

1. What are Global User IDs?

A global user ID is a common ID that uniquely identifies a particular user in a broad landscape, including various business applications and services from SAP. *1

In this system configuration without a global user ID, when accessing System B from System A, it was necessary to implement appropriate actions to know which user in each system was the same person as which user.

 

However, by introducing the concept and implementation of global user ID, you can avoid the complexity of ID mapping across systems. Technically, global user ID is a generated identifier in UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) or GUID (Global Unique Identifier) format, which is automatically assigned when a user is created in SAP Cloud Identity Services, Identity Authentication (aka SAP IAS, referred to below as SAP IAS).

This global user ID acts as a “correlation attribute” and acts as a back-to-back link between user accounts that exist in multiple systems.

For example, if an employee has a user account in both SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA, the global user ID uniquely connects the person. This allows other systems to know that certain actions are being done in relation to the same user, as well as to provide a consolidated reference to user information and tasks. Global user IDs are only internal, separate from the username and email address that end users enter when they log in, but they are a key element of system integration.

 

2. Why do I need a global user ID now?

The modern environment around SAP requires the integration of multiple cloud services and on-premise systems to provide a seamless experience for users. However, with disparate user IDs in each system, it is very difficult to have a corresponding relationship when integrating data and tasks for each user. So, by implementing a single identifier, the global user ID, it can be used effectively to address the challenge of integrating user-related data across system boundaries and to achieve consistent user identification across the enterprise. As a result, single sign-on (SSO) access to multiple SAP services with one authentication and consistent authorization management is possible.

One of the concrete use cases is the SAP Task Center. *2 The SAP Task Center is a service that consolidates workflow approval requests and tasks for users from various SAP applications (for example, SAP SuccessFactors) and displays them in a single inbox. The key here is user consistency and must be ensured that tasks coming from different back-end systems are tied to the same person. A global user ID serves as its common key and relates the tasks gathered from each system to the correct user. Without global user ID harmonization, complex logic is needed to match user IDs across systems, which can lead to missing or misallotted tasks.

 

 

Global user IDs are also important for use scenarios with Joule, our new AI digital assistant. Joule runs on the SAP Business Technology Platform and integrates with multiple back-end systems (such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP SuccessFactors) to support users by providing intelligent assistance and insights. *4

 

The assumption is that each system must be able to recognize that a user is the same person. Indeed, in order to activate Joule, there must be a global user ID for the same user on all products that work together. *5 In this way, global user identity is essential as an integrated authentication foundation for cross-sectional services like SAP Task Center and Joule, and is key to smoothly sharing user context across solutions.

 

3. Setup Overview

Here are the high-level steps for implementing Global User Identification and setting up integration with SAP IAS.

  1. Precondition Preparation: First, develop the system environment to take advantage of the Global User ID. Ensure that you have a tenant for SAP Cloud Identity Services and that each SAP product (for example, SAP SuccessFactors or SAP S/4HANA) is versioned for SAP IAS integration.

  2. Centralize and provision user identity; then design central management of user account information. Synchronize user information using tools such as SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning (commonly referred to as SAP IPS, referred to below as SAP IPS) to consolidate users that exist across different systems. For example, if you want to treat SAP SuccessFactors as the master of users, as an HR system, you can create and grant global user IDs at the same time by provisioning users to the SAP IAS user store via SAP IPS. SCIM provisioning in SAP IPS includes the userUuid attribute (Global User ID) in urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User, which is part of the SAP extension schema, so that all systems can have a common ID. *1
    In this way, provisioning users from the master source to SAP IAS provides each user with a unified global user ID, and the same identity can be deployed to other systems. In other SAP IPS usage scenarios, patterns such as the synchronization of user email addresses, global user IDs and group affiliations from SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Build Work Zone (Integrated Portal Service on SAP BTP) may also be taken.

  3. Trust configuration with SAP IAS: Configure trust relationships and user attribute mappings to ensure that SAP IAS is the authentication foundation (IdP) for each system. Specifically, open the SAML 2.0 configuration for each application (connected system) in the SAP IAS Administration Console and configure the attributes to be used for user identification. For authentication linkage with global user IDs, you must ensure that user_uuid (display name “Global User Id”) is included as one of the SAML assertion attributes on the SAP IAS side. If it does not exist by default, you must add the user_uuid attribute as a source with Identity Directory from the Add Custom Attribute feature and map the Global User ID to that value. *6 This setting ensures that when a user is authenticated with SAP IAS, the global user ID is included in the SAML token to uniquely identify the user on the integrated application side. Other attributes, such as Email and Login Name, are included in the assertion as needed, but the global user ID (user_uuid) is the key field for the cross-system user reconciliation process.

  4. Acceptance settings in each system: Finally, you make settings that map users received from SAP IAS correctly on the individual SAP product side. For SAP SuccessFactors, integration authentication with SAP IAS ensures that users in SAP SuccessFactors are associated with global user IDs (IDs generated on SAP IAS are mapped to SAP SuccessFactors users), so no additional configuration is required. Systems such as SAP S/4HANA also set up trust settings with SAP IAS for cloud products, and configure Global User IDs as Name IDs and attributes. In cases like connecting on-premise SAP S/4HANA to SAP Task Center, make sure that the Global User ID (SAPUSER_UUID) is available on the ABAP user management (e.g. SU01) and use it to link the user on the Task Center side. Although the configuration steps vary depending on the specific product, the key point is to ensure that each target system is configured to recognize users based on the Global User ID provided by SAP IAS.

 

4. Issues and considerations for the setup

The challenges and points to note when setting up Global User IDs and SAP IAS are summarized below.

  • Note the confusion of the identity concept: There are various IDs and numbers for each user in the SAP system (for example, user ID, employee ID, person ID, user GUID, and so on). Project members should be aware of the difference between the purpose of the Global User ID and other IDs, and avoid confusion due to similar names.

  • User synchronization and uniqueness: You must avoid the fact that each system issues a separate global user ID for the same user. Global user IDs are automatically generated when users are created in SAP IAS, but if you create the same person separately on different systems, for example, each has a separate global ID. To prevent this, you need to manage your users in central provisioning as described above, or connect them to a single user with a common key such as email address or employee ID when consolidating existing users. In particular, it is safe to first create a user on the SAP IAS side, issue a global user ID, and use it as a flow to link the user to other systems.

  • Wrong attribute mapping: User mapping inconsistencies occur if global user IDs are mapped incorrectly in the connection configuration between SAP IAS and the respective application. For example, if you forget to include user_uuid in the assertion on the SAP IAS side, or if the receiving side has a different attribute (email address, etc.) as the user key, the integration breaks across systems without considering the user as the same person. You should check carefully if the Global User ID is set to use as a user identification axis.

  • User management during production: After the introduction of a global user ID, user lifecycle management must also be kept in mind. When a user enters the company or leaves the company, verify that the user account addition and deletion process on SAP IAS (the Identity Directory feature, which is also the user master of) is properly synchronized with the user management on the part of each business system. For example, if an alumni account is deleted in one system but remains on SAP IAS, there is a risk that unnecessary access remains via a global user ID. In addition, if you are federating your corporate identity provider (e.g. Entra ID / Microsoft Active Directory) and SAP IAS, consider regular synchronization in SAP IPS or automatic provisioning setup on first login, as simply using SAP IAS in proxy mode does not automatically route global user IDs to each system.

 

Conclusion

You now have an overview of global user IDs and SAP IAS configuration points in the SAP environment. When using multiple SAP solutions in an integrated way, a common user foundation with a global user ID is key to success. Please refer to the official SAP Help Portal and the SAP Community guide article to design and configure for your landscape.

 

References

  • *1) SAP Help Portal: User Identity integration scenario with SAP Cloud Identity Services and its configuration guide
     https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity/system-integration-guide/global-user-id-in-integration-scenarios
  • *2) SAP Help Portal: What is the SAP Task Center?
    https://help.sap.com/docs/task-center/sap-task-center/what-is-sap-task-center? locale=en-US
  • *3) SAP Community Blog: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Step-by-Step Guide to Joule Setup Procedures
     https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/joule-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition-a-comprehensive-setup-guide/ba-p/13786453
  • *4) SAP Help Portal: What is Joule?
    https://help.sap.com/docs/JOULE/3fdd7b321eb24d1b9d40605dce822e84/38636457acc346daa4ff7069f041a11a.html
  • *5) SAP Help Portal: Prerequisites for working with SAP products
     https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf#:~:text=services%20tenant%20and%20within%20all,based%20application%20and%20relies%20on
  • *6) SAP Help Portal: Setup instructions for Joule Integration with SAP Cloud Identity Services
    https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf#:~:text=groups%20Groups%20user_uuid%20Global%20User,Save%20your%20configuration

entry.title: What's New in SAP BTP – Q1 2025
entry.content:
If you tuned in last year (or before), you probably remember our monthly bite-sized innovation highlight videos – SAP BTP Innobytes, where we showcased the coolest new features in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). Well, guess what? We’re back and better than ever in 2025, with even more enthusiasm and a demo-driven approach!

This year, we’ll be bringing you quarterly videos that dive deeper into SAP BTP innovations. While the videos will be a bit longer, they’ll be packed with demos to show these innovations in action and how they can help you achieve more.

To kick things off, we’re starting strong with our first video for Q1! Stay tuned for a closer look at all the exciting things we’ve been working on.

 

App Dev & Automation Highlights

Holistic Views Across Processes

For a holistic view across all processes, we have built dedicated Process Views for Requestors and Process Participants. These views provide information about running instances across multiple processes natively on top of Process or Workflow, without any external configuration.

Process Views has two views dedicated for Requestors and Process Participants named “My Requests” and “My Processes”.

"My Requests” application provides clarity to the Requestors on how their request fits into the workflow, the status of their request, and what actions (if any) they need to take as their request moves through the approval or fulfillment process.

The next application “My Processes” is dedicated to Process Participants like Approvers for example. Process Views help them easily track, understand, and make informed decisions about where their input is needed and how it fits into the larger workflow.

Process views are all about making complex workflows or progress status more digestible. It’s not just about having data; it’s about having the right type of view at the right time to make smarter decisions.

Watch the video demo (starting at 0:40) for more insights!

Learn more:

  • Register for the upcoming ‘What’s New in SAP Build’ webinar: https://sap.to/604200bfg

ABAP Meets SAP Build Code

Imagine seamlessly transitioning from the Cloud into the ABAP world. With SAP Build Code, that’s now a reality! In six easy steps you can integrate an ABAP remote function (RFC) into your Cloud Application Programming (CAP) project:

  1. Setup the CAP Application
  2. Explore Available Functions
  3. Add the ABAP Function to the Project
  4. Add Business Logic
  5. Implement the SAP Fiori UI
  6. Run the Application

Watch the video (starting at 2:16) to see these steps in action!

Learn more:

  • Explore SAP System Functions on the SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/build_code/d0d8f5bfc3d640478854e6f4e7c7584a/892114ce078b4e17a9ff7e751e6330cc.html#explore-sap-system-functions

Joule for Developers with ABAP

Are you looking for an assistant to help you build faster, smoother, and more efficiently? Look no further than Joule! In this demo (starting at 3:46), Sebastian explores how Joule can enhance your experience in ABAP Development Tools, particularly when it comes to efficient unit testing.

With Joule, you can easily identify and mock dependencies, as well as generate unit tests with minimal effort. This makes ABAP Cloud development significantly faster and more efficient, freeing you up to focus on more important tasks.

 Learn more:

  • Joule Speaks ABAP | SAP Community Blog Post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/joule-speaks-abap/ba-p/14018226

Artificial Intelligence Highlight

Reinventing Business Process with Joule and AI Agent Builder

AI agents are changing the game when it comes to business automation. These intelligent agents can plan, make decisions, and execute tasks based on user-defined goals, without the need for predefined processes. In this demo (starting at 5:06), Benjamin dives into how AI agents, powered by SAP’s Agent Builder on SAP BTP and Joule, are helping business users eliminate repetitive tasks and drive efficiency.

Learn more:

  • AI Agents on SAP.com: https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-agents.html

Integration Highlight

New Adapters for Cloud Integration

Today’s enterprises operate in a complex ecosystem of applications and platforms, which makes seamless integration more important than ever. With SAP Integration Suite, we make it easy to connect with over 250 applications, not just within SAP but also with a wide range of third-party systems.

We’re excited to announce the release of four new adapters for our Cloud Integration capability, which will help businesses streamline their integration processes even further. These adapters are now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

  1. IBM MQ Adapter
  2. Google Pub/Sub Adapter
  3. Azure CosmosDB Adapter
  4. Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter

These new adapters can be easily consumed via the Integration Flow designer. The first time you select an adapter, it’s automatically imported and deployed to your system—making setup both straightforward and efficient. From there, you can quickly connect to your target systems by simply providing the necessary connection details.

Take a closer look at the video (starting at 6:56).

Learn more:

  • SAP integration Suite – ‘Connect Everything’ Blog Series: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/sap-integration-suite-connect-everything/ba-p/13982527

Data & Analytics Highlight

Knowledge Graph Engine in SAP HANA Cloud

In today’s fast-paced world, making smart decisions requires working with highly connected, diverse, and ever-evolving data sets. SAP HANA Cloud’s Knowledge Graph Engine is here to help you tackle this challenge by improving data querying, generating deeper insights, and boosting the performance of AI agents. Explore how this powerful tool can transform the way you work with complex data in the video demo (starting at 8:02).

Learn more:

  • Connecting the Facts: SAP HANA Cloud’s Knowledge Graph Engine for Business Context SAP Community Blog Post

Cross Platform Highlight

SAP Automation Pilot Catalog for Cloud ALM Ops

The SAP Automation Pilot catalog provides instant access to ready-to-use automation commands, eliminating the need for your teams to manually execute repetitive tasks. These workflows are designed to integrate seamlessly with SAP Cloud ALM, ensuring the right context and commands are applied to the appropriate IT resources in your SAP landscape.

By automating routine operational tasks, SAP Automation Pilot significantly reduces the time spent on manual processes, freeing up valuable resources for more impactful work. Whether you’re managing incidents or performing system checks, SAP Automation Pilot helps take your SAP Cloud ALM operations to the next level.

Take a look at the demo (starting at 9:10).

Learn more:

  • “Building Technical Ops Automation Flows with GenAI (part of the learning journey: Discovering DevOps with SAP BTP )

Share your thoughts 

These videos are for YOU. We would love to hear your impressions. How do you like the video? How can we improve the upcoming videos? Please share your feedback by liking this post or leaving a comment below.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

You Might Also Be Interested In

  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos.
  • SAP BTP Talk Podcast: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP.

entry.title: Data Doesn’t Drive the Business - Trust Does. SAP Business Data Cloud Delivers.
entry.content:
Let’s face it - most companies are still wrestling with their data. It’s siloed, it’s messy, it’s buried under layers of integrations that no one wants to touch. If you’re a CIO, an architect, a data engineer, or frankly just someone trying to get answers from your dashboards without opening ten tabs… you’ve probably felt the pain. You know the one: pulling numbers from five different systems, reconciling definitions that “almost match,” or worse - waiting days for a report that turns out to be wrong. Again.

But here’s the kicker: it doesn’t have to be that way. And no, this isn’t a fairy tale. It’s what SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) was built for - making trusted, usable data available across the business, without the usual complexity tax.

Imagine this: business users get timely access to harmonized data with real business context. Data scientists spend less time cleaning and more time training actual models. IT stops duct-taping brittle pipelines together and starts focusing on delivering value. Oh, and everyone’s speaking the same data language. That’s the premise behind BDC: an open, connected, and context-rich data foundation that plays nice with your existing tools, simplifies your architecture, and makes trusted data products available wherever decisions happen. And it just got even more powerful thanks to SAP’s expanded partnership with Databricks.

Let’s unpack what that actually means - not in abstract terms, but in real, tangible outcomes.

Data that speaks the language of your business

Data on its own is… well, just noise. Rows in a spreadsheet, blobs in a lake. The real magic happens when that data is organized, harmonized, and contextualized - when it actually reflects how your business runs.

BDC does exactly that. It connects data from SAP and non-SAP systems alike, across cloud, hybrid, or even on-prem landscapes. It doesn’t care where the data lives - it brings it together without making a mess of things. It does this while preserving the meaning of your data. Sales orders stay sales orders. Materials remain materials. Because BDC keeps the business semantics intact - no translation layer required. Just usable, governed data products that are ready for analytics, AI, or whatever use case you throw at them.

And it doesn’t hurt that it’s open by design. With APIs, delta sharing, and zero-copy access, BDC plays nice with the tools you already use. Power BI? Tableau? Go for it.

Business Value?

  • Fewer manual workarounds and band-aid integrations
  • Less duplicated data flying around (and less compute waste)
  • A single source of truth that teams actually trust

Honestly, just that alone is a win.

AI doesn't work without good data

AI is all the rage - understandably so. But let’s not pretend models can fix bad data. Feeding garbage into your fancy new ML pipeline? That’s how you end up with expensive prototypes that go nowhere.

Here’s where BDC quietly shines. It embeds SAP’s business logic right into your data products. That means you’re not just training models on numbers - you’re training them on meaningful data, already cleaned, categorized, and structured with SAP’s One Domain Model.

Whether you're using SAP Databricks, Joule, or some other flavor of SAP Business AI, you're not starting from square one. You're starting with context, lineage, and trust.

Business Value?

  • Lower training costs (less data prep = less pain)
  • Fewer failed models because of missing or misunderstood inputs
  • More transparency and traceability (so you can actually explain your predictions)

It’s like building on bedrock instead of quicksand.

Dashboards at the speed of thought

We’ve all been there: You need a report. IT says it’ll take a week. By the time it arrives, the question has changed - and so has the market.

BDC changes the pace entirely. With prebuilt Insight Applications - like Working Capital Insights, Customer 360, and others - business users can answer questions on the fly. These aren’t brittle templates either. They’re powered by governed, reusable data models that are constantly updated and synced with live operations.

And because the semantics stay intact, what you see in the dashboard actually matches what’s happening in the business.

Business Value?

  • Less time waiting on IT for every report refresh
  • Faster decision-making, backed by trusted data
  • More empowered business teams, fewer bottlenecks

It’s what we call trusted self-service. And once teams get a taste, there’s no going back.

SAP + Databricks: Like peanut butter and (business process) jelly

Let’s talk partnerships. The SAP-Databricks collaboration is more than a handshake - it’s a unique integration that brings together SAP’s business logic with Databricks’ open lakehouse scalability.

Here's what that means in practice:

  • You can publish SAP data products directly into SAP Databricks Unity Catalog.
  • Those products carry over their semantics, governance, and lineage.
  • No data copying. No duct tape integrations. Just immediate availability.

And because the services are co-located in the same hyperscaler environment, there’s minimal latency. Your fraud detection models? They run faster. Your dashboards? They refresh in seconds.

Business Value?

  • No more translating SAP logic into custom pipelines (and re-translating when something breaks)
  • Less infrastructure overhead - and greater agility for your teams
  • Real-time responsiveness, even for heavy ML workloads

Oh, and did we mention it's serverless? Compute auto-scales. SAP handles storage. You focus on value, not VMs.

Unified security model

Security and compliance are usually the “yeah, we’ll deal with that later” part of data projects - until someone says the word audit.

With BDC, governance isn’t an afterthought. There's a unified control plane where admins can set policies, access rights, audit trails, and privacy controls across both SAP and Databricks environments. Set it once. Enforce it everywhere.

This matters. A lot.

Business Value?

  • Simplified security management across hybrid environments
  • Clear lineage and access logs for audits
  • Peace of mind that data isn’t slipping through the cracks

You’d be surprised how much goodwill a solid security posture earns with stakeholders (and regulators).

The future is built on data. Let’s build it together.

SAP Business Data Cloud isn’t a silver bullet - nothing is. But it’s a smart, strategic answer to problems we’ve all felt for years: too much data, too little trust, and too many silos. It simplifies what’s been painfully complex. It connects what’s long been fragmented. And it gives every team - business or technical - a way to act on data without the usual drama.

If you’re a SAP BW customer, don’t worry - you’re not being left behind. SAP offers a complimentary BW Modernization Assessment that gives you a clear, realistic path forward into this new world of governed, AI-ready data with SAP Business Data Cloud.

Not using BW? No problem. Reach out to your SAP account executive to start understanding how SAP Business Data Cloud can help you write your next chapter - with data that actually works for you. Alternatively, to learn more, visit our SAP Business Data Cloud product page.

entry.title: The Rise of Agentic AI: A Deep-Dive in the World of Multi Agent Systems
entry.content:
Introduction

Agents are the talk of the town. As per the predictions of top enterprise companies, Agentic AI is the highest priority in 2025 and forward. Still in early stages of evolution, the capabilities in reasoning and collaboration are breaking records every day. Soon agents are expected to be more independent and handle complete complex business tasks without human intervention.

What does this mean for developers, consultants and architects - A HUGE OPPORTUNITY.

Developing reliable Agents, is important and more critical is to ensure the agents collaborate efficiently to complete the desired tasks.

In my blog, I am going to explain concept of Agentic AI and deep dive into Multi Agent Systems. The intent of this blog is to provide you the starting point and give you a leverage to explore possibilities on your own and ofcourse, with the help of your preferred co-pilot!

Along with the theory, my intention is to showcase a fully functional prototype which you can refer as well - GitHub Repo.

So, buckle up! and let's start 🚀

I will breakdown the article into following parts

  1. Introduction to Agentic AI and Multi Agent Systems
  2. Key components of Agentic AI
  3. Agentic AI flow diagram
  4. Solution Architecture
  5. Code walkthrough
  6. Lessons learnt

Introduction to Agentic AI and Multi Agent System

Without much theory, I will explain my take on:

What is Agentic AI?

  1. AI systems where agents operate independently, taking their decisions and executing tasks without much human intervention.

  2. The agents in Agentic AI framework use tools to enhance the outcomes.

  3. Tools can be APIs, LLMs or documents which can provide data to the agents.

What is Multi Agent System?

  1. Multi Agent System is an implementation and/or extension of Agentic AI.

  2. More than one agents work together on specific tasks.

  3. These agents can also collaborate and delegate the tasks among themselves eventually leading to the final task completion.

Key Components of Agentic AI

In this learning journey, I have chosen autogen as the choice of agentic framework for developing the multi agent system. This doesn't mean that autogen can be used in all agentic scenarios. There are lot of agentic AI frameworks available, each of it having their own pros and cons. I will provide few market leading available frameworks at the end of this blog for you to explore.

Autogen heavily relies on LLM orchestration to collaborate between agents.

Autogen provides a ConversableAgent interface with three types of implementations

  1. AssistantAgent - This agent performs the task
  2. UserProxyAgent - This agent acts as a human proxy and reduces human intervention
  3. GroupChatManager - This agent acts as a manager to manage between agents and delegate the tasks

Below diagram gives a abstracted view of the different personas of agents in Autogen. Reference - https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/docs/Use-Cases/agent_chat/

Figure 1

Agentic AI Flow Diagram

In this section, I have created 2 simple flow diagrams showcasing how the agents work and interact with each other.

UserProxy and Assistant Agents in Action

The below diagram shows:

  1. The UserProxyAgent delegates the task to AssistantAgent.
  2. The AssistantAgent uses tools which are functions to enrich their outcome based on data.
  3. The AssistantAgent terminates the request once the expected output is achieved and responds to the user proxy agent.
  4. The UserProxyAgent provides the final response to the user.

Figure 2

UserProxy, GroupChatManager and Assistant Agents in Action

In this flow diagram, I have taken a finance use case where we have two AssistantAgents

  1. Finance Agent - Takes care of finance related queries.
  2. CSR Agent - Takes care of customer communication.

The GroupChatManager agent is the orchestrator to get the job done.

The below diagram shows:

  1. The UserProxyAgent delegates the task to GroupChatManager agent.
  2. The GroupChatManager agent delegates the request to either finance AssistantAgent or CSRAssistantAgent or both agents based on the user requests.
  3. The AssistantAgent uses tools which are functions to enrich their outcome based on data.
  4. The GroupChatManager agent terminates the request once the expected output is achieved and responds to the UserProxyAgent.
  5. The UserProxyAgent provides the final response to the user.

Figure 3

 

Solution Architecture

This section outlines a high level solution architecture.

  • The  Agentic AI application is deployed on Cloud Foundry runtime.
  • AI Launchpad  is leveraged to provide a wide set of model library available to choose.
  • The generative-ai-hub-sdk is plugged in to integrate with the underlying model of choice.
  • Backend APIs can be securely integrated leveraging Destination Service.
  • The Agentic AI can be consumed by any Chat Client (Joule for example) of choice as it is being exposed as REST API.

Figure 4

 Code Walkthrough

As you get the high level architecture, now it's time to deep dive into the code. The README file will help you get started. I would like to mention some important files in this section for you to give extra attention when you are browsing through the code:

  1. server.py - The main starting point of your application.
  2. llm_config.py - Central place to store all LLM configurations for all agents.
  3. prompt_config.py - Central place to store all prompts for the agents.
  4. aicoreclient.py - Custom model for LLM if you are not using openai LLM. 
  5. weather_agent.py - Autogen weather agent showcasing UserProxyAgent and AssistantAgent.
  6. finance_group_chat_agent.py - Autogen finance agent showcasing - UserProxyAgent, GroupChatManager and AssistantAgent.

For weather agent, I have integrated with real weather API.

For finance agent, I have mocked the backend calls with sample JSON

I have shared a fragment of log below which shows the agents communicating with each other. Take a look.

user_proxy (to chat_manager):

Can you send a text message to the customer CUST001 as a reminder for his pending invoice INV1005

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
finance_agent (to chat_manager):

***** Suggested function call: extract_customer_id *****
Arguments: 
{"user_input":"Can you send a text message to the customer CUST001 as a reminder for his pending invoice INV1005"}
********************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

>>>>>>>> EXECUTING FUNCTION extract_customer_id...
user_proxy (to chat_manager):

***** Response from calling function (extract_customer_id) *****
CUST001
****************************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
finance_agent (to chat_manager):

***** Suggested function call: fetch_customer_details *****
Arguments: 
{"customer_id":"CUST001"}
***********************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

>>>>>>>> EXECUTING FUNCTION fetch_customer_details...
user_proxy (to chat_manager):

***** Response from calling function (fetch_customer_details) *****
{'customer_id': 'CUST001', 'address': '123 Business St, New York, NY', 'account_manager': 'Michael Johnson', 'account_status': 'Active', 'company': 'Acme Corp', 'email': 'john.doe@acmecorp.com', 'phone': '+1-555-1234-1234'}
*******************************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
finance_agent (to chat_manager):

I have retrieved the details for customer CUST001. The phone number is +1-555-1234-1234.

Please proceed to send a text message to remind them about the pending invoice INV1005.

Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
csr_agent (to chat_manager):

***** Suggested function call: send_text_message *****
Arguments: 
{"phone_number":"+1-555-1234-1234","message":"Dear Acme Corp team, this is a friendly reminder about your pending invoice INV1005. Please ensure it is paid at your earliest convenience. Thank you."}
******************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

>>>>>>>> EXECUTING FUNCTION send_text_message...
******Text Message****** : +1-555-1234-1234
###########################################
Message: Dear Acme Corp team, this is a friendly reminder about your pending invoice INV1005. Please ensure it is paid at your earliest convenience. Thank you.

Lessons Learnt

  1. Prompts – Prompts are very important to get the desired output. Write your prompts meticulously. Learn prompt engineering.
  2. Latency – Agents can go in loops at times and increase overall time for response and cost of the request. Agent evaluation is a key.
  3. API calls – Choose your APIs wisely and consider the response times. Reduce multiple API calls.
  4. Heterogenous integrations – APIs are available in different styles - REST, ODATA, SOAP etc. Integrating with different APIs can be a challenge.
  5. Focus – Keep your agents focussed on one particular task. If the task is going to take longer time then better to handle it asynchronously.

Available Agentic AI Frameworks

I have listed current few agentic AI frameworks available and are good starting point to start learning and developing your multi agent systems.

  1. LangGraph - https://www.langchain.com/langgraph 
  2. CrewAI - https://www.crewai.com
  3. Semantic Kernel - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/semantic-kernel/ 

Conclusion

Drawing from my experience, I must emphasize that these insights can serve as a starting point, though their applicability may vary based on specific scenarios. This project will surely give you a holistic view from design and development of Agentic AI solutions and eventually deployment on cloud in production scenarios.

Take a deep breath and start coding  —  GitHub Repo

Stay curious! Keep learning!

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my experience and learnings about Agentic AI.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/project/autogen/ 
  2. https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/docs/tutorial/introduction
  3. https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/blog/2024/01/26/Custom-Models 
  4. https://github.com/microsoft/autogen/issues/2929

entry.title: So this is it: I’m VERPed!
entry.content:
📢  Today is my last day working for SAP.

Indeed, I've been accepted into the very last round of the Voluntary Early Retirement Plan launched by SAP France in 2024, for which I applied the day of my 62nd birthday, on March 10.

It has been quick, but I'm happy. I thanks my management hierarchy Diana Roesner / André Borchert / Thomas Volmering / Steffen Pietsch / Michael Ameling / Muhammad Alam / Christian Klein (*) for giving me the unique opportunity to stop working 33 months earlier than expected! 😊

(*) yes, I was very low in SAP org chart 😉 but I do not care: what matters to me is to bring my expertise where and when it makes a real impact, and I humbly think it was the case during my 6.5-year tenure in this great company. 

I will really miss my wonderful team of such lovely colleagues in charge of SAP Build Product Management, Enablement and Communications 🥹  ... but I will still be hanging out here on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to follow what they will be doing with SAP Build and broadly SAP Business Technology Platform with Artificial Intelligence and Joule, because I know what will be announced in a few weeks at SAP Sapphire 2025, and for sure they will rock the stage!

 

I would like to heartfully thank Diana Roesner, André Borchert, Thomas Volmering, Michael Ameling, Sebastian Schrötel, Bhagat Nainani, Juergen Mueller and all the colleagues for having supporting me during my 6+ years at SAP, during this amazing journey from SAP Intelligent RPA to SAP Build. 🙏🏻

👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓 I seize this opportunity to promote 4️⃣ learnings on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Build.

Indeed, I won't spoil anything but there will be tremendous announcements in a few weeks: leverage these learnings to be prepared!

✅ An Enterprise Architect’s View on SAP Business Technology Platform

✅ Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build

✅ Compose and Automate with SAP Build the No-Code Way

✅ Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

📖  Let me also recommend the SAP Build Process Automation book from SAP PRESS!

Order this book now and learn how to create processes, automations, actions, and decisions by following step-by-step instructions with this expert-written guide by my dear colleagues @ArchanaShukla, @Chaitanya_Priya Puvvada, @Stephan Schluchter, Seshadri Sreenivas, and Steven Jacobson.

Finally, I would like to say that I'm very happy because I will spend 2 days in Paris April 14-15, 2 days in Walldorf May 12-13 and finally 2 days in Berlin May 14-15 to hang out with my colleagues and say a warm and grateful goodbye: if you will be there, come say hi! 🥲

And now, for me it's time to…

 

 💙 I wish you all the very best! 💙

entry.title: 1H 2025 New and Upgraded Transactional Use Cases in Joule in Talent space
entry.content:
New Transactional Use Cases in Joule

Performance & Goals:
1.Create Activity – Allows users to create activities for themselves and for others that they share a channel with. Users can also link the activities to relevant performance and development goals.
2.View Performance Goals – Access and view detailed performance goals, including name, plan, and status. Navigate to the goal details for more insights.
3.Edit Performance Goals – Edit performance goals by adjusting status and completion percentage. Currently only these two fields are supported.
4.Add Comments to Performance Goals – Facilitate collaboration and feedback by adding comments to performance goals.

Succession & Development:
1.View Development Goals – View development goals, including the goal name, goal plan name, and goal status, and follow a link to the goal details page for more information.
2.Edit Development Goals – Allows users to edit the status and completion percentage of development goals. Currently only these two fields are supported.
3.Add Comments to Development Goals – Allows users to add comments to development goals.

Upgraded Existing Transactional Use Cases in Joule


Opportunity Marketplace:
1.Approve assignment offers
2.Approve assignment acceptance
3.Approve assignment applications
4.Approve assignment publishing

Performance & Goals:
1.Give feedback
2.Request feedback
3.Edit performance goals

Succession & Development:
1.View talent pool nominee approvals
2.Approve mentors
3.Approve mentoring requests
4.View and edit development goals
5.Add comments to development goals

For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

Thanks

Mridul

entry.title: Sales Innovation with SAP S/4HANA, Business AI, and User Experience: A Practical Approach
entry.content:
From the perspective of a sales representative, in today's fast-paced companies, they must multitask all the time to keep up with the demands of their jobs, including nurturing connections with customers, negotiating and completing deals, and monitoring key performance indicators. With the help of business AI, the digital assistant Joule, and the new SAP S/4HANA system, sales representatives are now able to accomplish more in less time. In this article, we'll look at how SAP S/4HANA boosts sales rep efficiency, solves common business problems, and puts SAP ahead of the competition.

The Routine of a Salesperson Using SAP S/4HANA

Just picture yourself as a salesperson beginning each day with a synopsis of the most pressing matters that need your attention right away. This is made very easy and feasible using SAP S/4HANA. Important tasks, forthcoming meetings, and awaiting approvals are highlighted on a personalized dashboard made possible by the platform's user-friendly design. This clear view of your priorities allows you to start each day on a positive note.

Harnessing AI for Your Company

Integrating with advanced business AI is one of SAP S/4HANA's distinguishing characteristics. This technology helps sales representatives make informed decisions by analysing large volumes of data and providing actionable insights. To illustrate the point, AI can determine which leads have the best chance of converting, so you can direct your attention to the most promising ones. Furthermore, it can detect patterns and trends in consumer behaviour, letting you adjust your sales tactics appropriately.

Navigating SAP S/4HANA with Ease

The intuitive design of SAP S/4HANA makes navigating the system a joy. There is less of a learning curve for new users on the site because of how it is designed. With SAP S/4HANA, you can easily update customer information, generate sales reports, and track your performance KPIs. Focus on what matters most - creating relationships and closing business - with the help of the effortless navigation that saves time and boosts productivity.

Have a look at Joule: Your Personal Digital AI Assistant
The digital AI assistant Joule, which is a part of SAP S/4HANA, is going to revolutionize the way sales personnel do their jobs. When you use Joule, it's like having your own personal assistant that streamlines your work process. Would you like to set up a meeting? No problem for Joule. Are you in search of the most recent sales data? Get up-to-the-minute information with Joule. You can focus on more strategic endeavors with the time you gain from using Joule to automate mundane chores.

Dealing with Problems Facing the Company
Managing client connections, monitoring sales success, and keeping up with market developments are a few of the common challenges that sales professionals encounter. Let’s follow the system and navigate to understand this better way. Here Jessica Walker acts as a sales representative. Let's start the demo - 

I am Jessica Walker, a sales representative, and I commence my day by examining tasks and notifications that want prompt attention.

Fig 1 Task & Situations

 Rather than navigating multiple reports and dashboards, I review "My Home" for a crisp overview of urgent tasks and notifications. Thereby addressing the following task.

- Approving  a quotation that requires immediate attention.
- Examine situation notifications regarding the uploaded incomplete PDF and assess the deficient data.

Fig 2 Incomplete SO

Clicking on notifications directs me to the impacted sales order. The status indicates "Data Incomplete." A notification bell captures my attention.

Fig 3: Item Level SO error

I promptly review the warning and discover that the system has already diagnosed the issue and suggested a solution.

Fig 4 : AI guided Explaination

Upon the accurate update of data, the system permits a side-by-side display for verification.

Fig 5: PDF comparisonThe data has been updated, and save it for subsequent processing.

Fig 6: Udate Missing data

Then I scroll down and observe many "Insight Business Cards." One of them is "Sales Order Blocked." It is also displaying an itemized list of sales orders. As a sales representative, I am accountable for the seamless execution of the sales cycle. I opt to examine a single sales order from the list to understand the underlying details.

Fig 7: Insight Card "Delivery Block"Fig 8: Remove Delivery BlockFig 9 : Delivery Block Removed

 From the "Insight Card," I swiftly transition to the sales order and discover it is in a blocked status. Upon comprehensive examination, I remove the block and allow it to proceed further.

Fig 10: Updated insight card

Upon returning to the "Home Page," the affected sales order is no longer visible in the insight card.

Now I subsequently return to the homepage. I  have a customer meeting scheduled for later this week. I seek a summary of recent customer activities and an analysis of significant financial transactions and contracts in preparation for the meeting. I use Customer 360 to obtain this information, providing me with a comprehensive overview for the customer.

Fig 11: Customer 360Fig 12 : Customer 360 : Business TransactionsFig 13: Customer 360 : All Transaction view

I intend to verify specific customer facts through the "Customer 360 View." I leverage "Smart Filtering" to identify specific customers from an extensive list. All consumer details are now before me. I can examine every minute detail of it. I utilize “AI—Smart Summarization” to extract all important facts about that customer.  I want to communicate this information to the  management for weekly meeting.

Fig 14 : AI summary for productFig 15: AI summary for Sales

A customer contacts me regarding an inquiry concerning a sales order, providing the "customer reference" number. (It is ZXW-560 in this case.) I can utilize the launchpad search within an application to promptly locate the business object.

Fig 16: Customer ReferenceFig 17: Result of Customer reference

The system retrieves the precise order through efficient search and presents it for inspection.

For my upcoming task, I intend to seek assistance from the AI-powered chatbot named "Joule." I am mostly engaged in sales activities, but due to the sick leave of the return clerk , I have to take care of that part too. I receive a return from a customer but lack familiarity with the return creation procedure. I use Joule to ascertain the method for facilitating a return effortlessly.

Fig 18: JouleFig 19: Joule for Customer returnFig 20: Create Return

I have initiated a chat with "Joule". I seek assistance with "return order management" and am requesting guidance from "Joule." Joule is providing comprehensive information, including the application name, direct navigation, and assistance features. I have accessed "Manage Customer Return" through direct navigation from Joule.

However, there are numerous fields that remain unfamiliar to me. I request assistance from "X-Ray" to comprehend certain fields and their values. Subsequently, I observe that the system has initiated AI-driven auto-recommendations for return orders. This feature facilitates a seamless return creation process.

Fig 21: Help recomendationFig 22: X Ray helpFig 22: Auto RecomendationFig 23: Choose RecomendationFig 24: Save & Release

Upon the creation of a return order, I wish to pass on its data via Microsoft Teams to another team member for subsequent processing. The returns clerk receives a Microsoft Teams notice on their mobile device for the next action.

Fig 25 : Share in Microsoft TeamsFig 26: Display in MS card

This step concludes the system navigation/demo. 

SAP S/4HANA tackles these problems directly:
You can see your clients from every angle using SAP S/4HANA, which is great for CRM (customer relationship management). You may better comprehend their requirements, communicate with them on a personal level, and develop more profound relationships.
2. Sales Performance Tracking: With the platform's real-time analytics and reporting, you can keep tabs on how well you're doing compared to your goals. You can quickly pinpoint problem areas and implement solutions with this level of transparency.
3. Up-to-Date Market Trends and Insights: The business AI in SAP S/4HANA is always analyzing market data to bring you the most recent trends and insights. If you want to keep ahead of the competition and adjust your sales tactics to fit the ever-changing industry, you need this information.

Advantages for Clients
Customers, not just salespeople, may reap the benefits of SAP S/4HANA. Customers are guaranteed fast and personalized service with SAP S/4HANA's help, thanks to its ability to streamline sales processes and enhance efficiency. The software keeps clients informed about the status of their orders and any potential concerns through real-time updates and analytics. This openness cultivates trust and encourages loyal patronage in the long run.

An epic shift for SAP business artificial intelligence (AI), intuitive user interface (UI), and digital assistant Joule all come together in SAP S/4HANA, making it a revolutionary product in the corporate software industry. A key differentiator for SAP S/4HANA is its ability to alleviate sales agents' problems while simultaneously increasing client happiness. Salespeople can remain busy, educated, and responsive because to the platform's easy design and powerful features, which drive corporate success.
To sum up, SAP S/4HANA is revolutionizing the day-to-day work of sales reps. Its digital assistant Joule, user-friendly UI, and incorporation of business AI make it an essential tool for sales teams. Sales agents work more efficiently with SAP S/4HANA, and the company solidifies its position as an industry leader in enterprise software by alleviating typical business pain points and increasing customer satisfaction. Without a question, SAP S/4HANA will be pivotal in determining how sales operations are shaped in the future as firms undergo continuous evolution.

 

For more information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, check out the following links:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud release info here
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Early Release Series Webinars here
  • Latest 2502 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release Blogs here

  • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud here
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Community: here
  • Feature Scope Description here
  • What’s New here
  • Help Portal Product Page here
  • Implementation Portal here

 

Please Check out my latest Blogs on SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

 

All AI Blogs :

  • Sales Innovation with SAP S/4HANA, Business AI, and User Experience: A Practical Approach
  • Harnessing Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA Sales Order Extraction from PDF
  • Optimizing Procurement Processes with Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP S/4HANA Central Invoice Ma...
  • Enhancing Sales Efficiency-—AI-Powered Sales Order Autocomplete in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition...
  • Boost Sales Order Efficiency with Joule, SAP’s AI-powered Copilot
  • Streamline Customer Return Management with SAP Business AI

 

All End to end Journey Blogs:

  • E2E journey of Make to Stock (MTS) Logistics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Make-to-Order (MTO) with Advanced Variant Configuration Process with Embedded **bl...
  • A Deep Dive into SAP S/4HANA Cloud’s End-to-end Solution for Service Orders, Subscription Billing, a...


Special Functionalities/Scenario Blogs:

  • Capabilities of Solution Order Management in S/4HANA Cloud, public edition 2302 release


AATP Blogs:

  • Configuration & Business Case of SAP S/4HANA Advanced ATP (AATP) with Business Process Scheduling (B...

 

Follow me on Mrinal Kanti Chanda & LinkedIn

entry.title: Product Inspiration Series - Experience a New Era of SAP CX
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We're happy to inform you that our Product Inspiration Series for Intelligent CX continues in 2025, with a new set of deep dive videos featuring most recent innovations from our SAP product teams and ISV partners. According to our AI-first and suite-first strategy those videos aim to inspire you on what's possible in the new era of AI agents and seamlessly connected end-to-end processes across SAP Business Suite. 

Thereby the special character of this video series remains unchanged. Each video...

  • covers specific innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio,
  • is made from product or industry experts for experts,
  • typically starts with a short Q&A at the beginning (why, what, how),
  • does not contain any slides (zero slides rule),
  • consists of deep-dive and/or process demos,
  • wants to inspire you by showcasing real software,
  • is produced in 4K to offer a jaw-dropping viewing experience,
  • helps you understand the Consumer Industries & CX product strategy better, 
  • is made with love and dedication for you - our valued customers, partners, colleagues or simply CX enthusiasts.

But now let's have a closer look at the first videos which made it through the finish line...

1. Predictive Planning and Inventory Orchestration from SAP

2. Extensibility of SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 using No Code / Low Code Tools

3. Sinch Omnichannel Customer Engagement for SAP CX

4. Open Payment Framework for SAP Commerce Cloud in Action

5. Open Search Service for SAP Commerce Cloud in Action (coming soon)

6. Business Information Extraction for SAP Enterprise Service Management (coming soon)

 

7. Intelligent Selling with Joule in SAP Sales Cloud and S/4HANA Cloud in Action (coming soon)

If you have any questions or want to share your feedback please let me know. 

Further videos will be published soon. 🚀

Enjoy and have fun.

entry.title: AI Agents in Action
entry.content:
Introduction

As generative AI continues to transform business operations, AI agents are emerging as key players, enhancing collaboration and redefining efficiency. Building on my previous blog post, I now explore three compelling use cases of AI agents in action to demonstrate how AI agents streamline customer service, cut the time it takes to complete customer dispute resolutions time and how to easily build and customize AI agents with no-code workflows.

What are AI Agents? Watch the video from Jonathan to find out more.

Before I deep dive into AI agent use cases, let’s first clarify when it actually makes sense to build an AI agent use case?

Implementing AI agents in business processes can significantly enhance efficiency and decision-making when applied to suitable tasks. Key criteria for determining the appropriateness of AI agents include:

  • Processing Unstructured Data: AI agents excel at analyzing and organizing unstructured data – such as emails, documents, or tickets – transforming it into structured formats. This capability enables businesses to extract valuable insights and automate subsequent actions based on the classifications. For example, AI can automatically categorize customer inquiries and route them to the appropriate department, creating case documentation and subsequent actions and thus enhancing response times and customer satisfaction.

  • Enhancing Decision-Making with Data Analysis: AI agents can process large volumes of data to identify patterns and trends that might be overlooked by human analysis. This supports informed decision-making in areas like market analysis, risk assessment, and strategic planning. By providing data-driven recommendations, AI agents empower businesses to make proactive and effective decisions and make business priority the key criteria for the users’ attention.
     
  • Automating Complex Repetitive Tasks with Contextual Understanding: Unlike traditional Robotic Process Automation (RPA) systems that handle straightforward, rule-based tasks, AI agents can manage repetitive tasks requiring nuanced understanding and minor contextual decisions. This allows for the automation of long-tail business processes previously unsuitable for automation. For instance, AI agents can interpret customer feedback, discern sentiment, and initiate appropriate follow-up actions, thereby enhancing customer engagement and operational efficiency.

These criteria should of course not be considered exhaustive, but by evaluating your business processes against these criteria, organizations can strategically implement AI agents to automate tasks, enhance accuracy, and drive growth.

Let’s have a look at some agentic use cases and how they are transforming organizational efficiency across lines of businesses

How AI Agents Streamline Customer Service

Normally, when a customer service ticket comes in,  customer services teams start manually classifying the case using basic routing rules – often spending valuable time just getting tickets to the right team. Next, service representatives spend significant time searching documentation and reviewing similar cases – often across multiple systems. When they reach a resolution, they draft responses and might update the knowledge base if they remember and have time. And here's where missed opportunities frequently occur – in the sales handoff phase. When potential sales opportunities are identified and created, unfortunately, sales teams often have to ask customers the same questions again because context gets lost in the handoff. This process works, but many customers find it time-consuming, nerve-racking, and prone to mistakes.

Now, let’s explore how this process is being transformed with AI agents. Each stage is now enhanced with intelligent automation while the customer service team remains in control. The Classification Agent handles smart categorization and instant routing based on business rules. It's like having an expert agent who knows all your routing protocols working 24/7. To find solutions, the Q&A Agent automatically searches the knowledge base and matches solutions – imagine having the  most experienced agent's knowledge instantly available.  In the resolution stage, the Knowledge Agent not only documents the solution but intelligently captures customer needs and usage patterns. This is crucial because it feeds into the next stage. Finally, the same Q&A Agent that helped with service, now provides immediate answers to any product or upgrade questions included in the opportunity notes. No more repeated questions or lost opportunities.

What's powerful about this new process is that it maintains all the context and customer intelligence throughout the journey, while automating the time-consuming tasks that previously slowed  teams down. Teams can focus on building customer relationships while the AI handles the heavy lifting of information gathering and routing.

These AI agents are delivered through the CX AI Toolkit and work out of the box with SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud, enhancing the processes and tools customers already use. CX agents are generally available today, and they offer new workflows to enhance the typical sales and service process within your SAP environment.  

Check out the demo to see what this looks like within the SAP Service Cloud: https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2025/01/b8dba82e-f27e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

How AI Agents Cutting Dispute Resolution Time

Let’s explore another scenario which involves multiple agents simplifying the most complex tasks and processes using AI. You will see how proactive agents can monitor and react faster than any human.

Picture this: you’re an accounts receivable clerk, responsible for receiving and processing customer queries daily. Balancing the need to collect money with maintaining customer satisfaction is a tedious, time-consuming process. Now, the good news is: agents can help collect this money more quickly.  

In this scenario, let's explore how a customer experience agent communicates directly with the customer, while a cash flow agent coordinates the entire process. These agents operate seamlessly within the SAP system and collaborate in the background with Microsoft Copilot.

An accounts receivable clerk receives an urgent customer issue via a Microsoft Teams notification. The integrated AI system immediately jumps into action, outlining the steps the agents have taken: reading the customer’s email, retrieving pertinent information, creating a dispute proposal, and more. The clerk can easily view the invoice by accessing it in SAP S4/HANA Cloud.

To understand the concrete next steps, the clerk asks the SAP AI copilot, Joule, to provide a detailed update. The collection agent communicates with multiple other agents – customer service, financial accounting, and shipping and receiving – to gather the necessary information, and then the final recommendation is displayed. This process would usually take hours, and now requires only a few seconds. 

Furthermore, Joule not only presents the resolution but also the logic, reasoning, and sources behind it. The clerk can see the various data sources, objects, and documents that were used as part of this orchestration to come up with the resolution. Additionally, Joule provides two alternative suggestions, offering a comprehensive view of potential solutions. Let’s suppose the clerk decides to accept the resolution and to select goodwill option B. With a simple command, the system puts the decision into action, with the agents working together to create and send the necessary email using a large language model (LLM). This being said: The human always has the final say. Now looking at the dispute case that's been created in the system it’s documented that the disputed amount is settled and the dispute is closed – what a success! 

After making sure the customer stays happy, the next task is addressing the issue caused by the third-party shipping company that failed to deliver on time. By asking Joule for guidance, the clerk can quickly determine the best course of action.

Joule's agents analyze the situation and conclude that the shipping company clearly violated the service level agreement. The agents promptly recommend opening a dispute case. With just one click, the dispute case is created and sent off to the shipper.

A process that would usually take days or weeks has been solved in just a few minutes – all thanks to the collaborative efforts of the AI agents. This level of efficiency and effectiveness is what makes working with these agents so powerful and beneficial.

Watch the video for more: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1Ku7J4XIfE8

Easily Build and Customize AI Agents with No-Code Workflows.

Every business and industry has unique requirements, and creating custom agents that suit your organization is now straightforward using Joule Studio in SAP Build.

For instance, if the business is growing and you need to renegotiate supplier contracts for increased volume, you can create a new Joule agent to develop strategies that support your negotiations.

Let me walk you through the steps to create a new agent from scratch!

To begin, you'll define the agent’s purpose, for example, helping procurement managers negotiate better pricing and terms. Joule guides you through this process, and you’ll name your agent something fitting, like "Pricing Negotiator." Joule then takes us to the build page and fills out the basic information. It analyses the description and from this generates an avatar and a banner image.

Next, the bounds of the agent’s role can be set by assigning it to a business process, using the to identify the correct sub-process within the source-to-pay process. Collaboration is crucial, so the Pricing Negotiator is connected to other relevant agents. Adding third-party agents like Microsoft 365 Copilot is also straightforward, requiring just one simple click.

Data is essential to ensure the Joule agent is grounded in business context. Using the SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Knowledge Graph, relevant data products such as contracts, invoices, and payment history can be easily identified and added. Externally sourced information, like third-party benchmarking, can also be seamlessly integrated.

The Pricing Negotiator is then equipped with the necessary tools by giving it access to functional libraries of Joule skills, such as invoice management and supplier search.

We need to define when the Pricing Negotiator will be active – this is done via triggers. We have multiple options here, the default is calling it directly from Joule but we could create a schedule or link it to another process.

Triggers are defined to determine when the agent will be active, and communication channels for human collaboration, such as Microsoft Teams, are set up. Joule summarizes the agent’s objectives and tools, allowing for the addition of constraints, such as not negotiating with suppliers overdue for more than 90 days until deliveries are resolved.

To enhance performance, a reasoning model is used to help the agent make more nuanced judgments. Finally, the agent is tested by entering a prompt like “Look up the last RFQ issued to our supplier Best Run and create a negotiation strategy.” The Joule agent starts to reason, plan, and act – using all the business context, tools, and other agents as needed to decide on the best strategy, including key negotiation tactics, risks, and even alternatives! 

And right at the end of it, there’s a concise summary. 

Creating custom Joule agents is a straightforward process that significantly enhances business operations by making teams more efficient and streamlining workflows.

Watch the Joule Agent Builder demo here: https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2025/02/4212ffbc-f27e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

Let’s now briefly touch on Responsible AI, the core of SAP’s mission.

As you read earlier, the human always has the final say. Responsible AI is a critical priority for SAP because it aligns with the company’s broader values of trust, transparency, and sustainability, which are central to our role as a leader in enterprise software. Consequently, Joule Agents are designed with a human-in-the-loop (HITL) framework, ensuring that human oversight is always maintained in AI-driven operations. This approach allows humans to review and validate AI decisions, achieving transparency and enhancing reliability. Users have the final authority to approve or modify AI recommendations, reducing errors and incorporating expert judgment.

AI Ethics and Governance for Agentic AI emphasize several crucial areas:

  1. Human Oversight: Ensuring human judgment is integral to decision-making.
  2. Transparency and Explainability: Making AI processes clear and understandable.
  3. Fairness and Non-Discrimination: Preventing biased outcomes, especially in applications affecting individuals.

The HITL approach is essential in use cases that:

  • Process Personal Data or Personal Identifiable Information
  • Potentially Impact Individuals Negatively
  • Operate in High-Risk Domains (e.g., HR, critical infrastructure, law enforcement).

By adhering to these principles and structures, businesses can ensure that AI agents are used responsibly and ethically. The integration of responsible AI practices includes human oversight, collaboration, and adherence to ethical guidelines, ensuring efficient, ethical, and transparent AI operations.

By now, it’s clear that AI agents are intended to amplify and accelerate the work of humans. As they reshape the way we work, they accelerate the execution of complex tasks and augment decision-making, allowing human workers to focus on more strategic activities that require human insight and creativity. As with any technological shift, the key is to embrace AI as a collaborator, leveraging it to enhance productivity and innovation, rather than viewing it as a threat. Businesses that invest in upskilling their workforce will unlock the full potential of AI while ensuring employees remain at the center of decision-making. This relationship between humans and AI agents fosters productivity and innovation, reflecting a forward-thinking approach to modern work environments.

Speaking of upskilling! Make sure to engage with our newly released Learning Journey “Boosting AI-Driven Business Transformation with Joule Agents”. After completion, you will be able to understand and utilize Joule Agents to drive business transformation, enhance decision-making, and integrate AI capabilities.
